agent_notes: Croydon has a real cultural scene but a narrow PAINTS-compliant venue base under v6: several good-sounding bars have closed, Books & Coffee is especially thin, and only Farthing Downs plus Crystal Palace Park clear the approved Creative Walk safety list.

Opening hours, walk-in tolerance for groups of 8-12, and Google rating/review counts still need host confirmation before publishing; where not found in live official sources, fields are marked UNVERIFIED.

Turf Projects has a current Zine Mela programme on 3-30 July 2026 and a stronger official exhibition, Prefab Paradise, opening 11 July 2026; use the Turf pick from 11 July onward unless the host confirms Zine Mela access for PAINTS.

venues.opening_hours: Official current programme: Zine Mela listed 3-30 July 2026 via ArtRabbit/Instagram; official next exhibition Prefab Paradise opens 11 July 2026 and continues to 26 September 2026. Standard public hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery-style walk-in likely for public exhibitions, but group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for a small hosted gallery visit; workshop/event capacity and quiet discussion space UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Artist-run contemporary art space in Whitgift Centre; group flow should be checked because exhibitions and workshops can be compact.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 12:00-14:00 or Wednesday-Friday late morning, avoiding opening launch crowds.
venues.why_fits_paints: Croydon's first entirely artist-run contemporary art space gives PAINTS a non-central, locally made cultural anchor. The July programme leans into zines, speculative futures, model-making and immersive practice rather than passive white-wall viewing. It is independent, discussion-friendly and materially connected to Croydon's creative regeneration story.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded in 2013 by Croydon locals, Turf is described by Croydon BID as the borough's homegrown artist space and first entirely artist-run contemporary art space.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Current/near-current: Zine Mela: South Asian Journeys in DIY Cultures, 3-30 July 2026; Prefab Paradise by Amy J Wilson and Meitao Qu opens 11 July 2026, blending miniature dioramas and immersive technology.
venues.safety_notes: Town-centre daytime gallery visit with strong transit access; use East Croydon/Church Street tram routes and avoid late-night dispersal through low-footfall Whitgift approaches.
venues.risks_watchouts: The Whitgift Centre setting is culturally interesting but operationally fragile; host should verify exact public entrance, exhibition install dates and group access before listing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says free entry and open Wednesdays-Saturdays; current exhibitions: Forever We Can Be Full, 27 May-31 August 2026, and Creative Health, 19 June-22 August 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum visit, walk-in generally expected; timed/group policy for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a compact hosted group if galleries are not busy; group visit arrangements UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Clocktower galleries; best with a clear meet point in the foyer and a short hosted route rather than a long dwell in one room.
venues.why_fits_paints: The museum turns Croydon's civic history into a conversation starter rather than a generic local-history stop. The current refugee youth and creative health exhibitions have a strong social-practice angle that fits PAINTS' meaningful-connection brief. It is free, central and easy to pair with Turf Projects.
venues.anecdote_story: Forever We Can Be Full marks 10 years of Croydon-based charity Play for Progress and gathers original artwork, photography, music, poetry and testimony by young refugees and asylum seekers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Forever We Can Be Full; Creative Health exhibition about arts, culture and wellbeing projects supporting residents.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime civic-building visit in central Croydon; host should direct attendees to East Croydon or tram exits after the event.
venues.risks_watchouts: Press depth is local rather than national; the exhibition is meaningful but less visually polished than major London institutions.

venues.opening_hours: Croydon Council describes a daily market dating back over 700 years; Facebook page lists Monday-Saturday 8:00-17:00. Sunday/artisan return is not sufficiently verified for PAINTS.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a hosted wander if kept to daytime and split into pairs at stalls; no single seated base.
venues.capacity_notes: Best treated as a route with a fixed meeting point, stall prompts, and a nearby cafe/pub debrief.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:30-12:30, when street food/artisan energy is more plausible than weekday produce-only trading.
venues.why_fits_paints: Surrey Street gives Croydon texture and history rather than a polished lifestyle market. Its 700-year trading story creates instant conversation, and its central location makes it easy for strangers to arrive. It is Tier B because the current offer can be uneven and weather-dependent.
venues.anecdote_story: Croydon Council cites the market as over 700 years old; histories trace market rights to medieval archbishops and the street remains one of Croydon's defining public spaces.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fruit, vegetables, street food, clothing, crafts and rotating traders; exact Saturday trader mix UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; avoid late afternoon dispersal if footfall drops. Keep the group on the market spine and main tram/rail routes.
venues.backup_venue: Clocktower Market SE25 or Crystal Palace Food Market
venues.backup_walk_time: No same-category backup within 15 min walk; closest qualifying alternatives require tram/rail transit, approx. 15-25 min depending on service.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup rule is the binding constraint, so this is capped at Tier B; current trader mix and group comfort vary by day.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Upper Norwood / Crystal Palace or Gipsy Hill
venues.opening_hours: Croydon Council lists every Saturday 10:00-15:00; market site describes a weekly Saturday market with 16 weekly permanent stalls, visiting stalls and an on-site licensed cafe.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 as a loose browsing route with a fixed meeting point; seating depends on cafe/yard availability.
venues.capacity_notes: Narrow-lane market; keep the group moving and reserve longer conversations for cafe/pub backup.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the most PAINTS-coded market in the borough: local, sustainable, maker/producer-led and tucked down Haynes Lane rather than overexposed. The mix of permanent stalls, visiting makers and an on-site cafe gives it enough structure for a hosted social event. It also pairs naturally with Crystal Palace's bookshop and park culture, even though the bookshop-cafe rule does not clear.
venues.anecdote_story: The market presents itself as a Transition Town market supporting sustainable farming and locally produced food, with neighbours including Haynes Lane vintage market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fish, meat, bread, fruit, street food, visiting stalls, and an on-site licensed cafe; weekly lineup changes.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Upper Norwood route with good local footfall; hosts should avoid sending solo attendees through quiet park edges after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min walk/transit depending on park entrance; category backup is Sunday rather than Saturday, so host must confirm date fit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and crowding in the lane; backup operates on a different day, so publishing requires date-specific confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Croydon Council says the market is monthly on the second Saturday; Instagram says reopening late Sept/early Oct, 10:00-16:00. Not usable until reopening is confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for a light community-market format when active; current operating status prevents Tier A.
venues.capacity_notes: Street/community market format around Station Road; no guaranteed seated base.
venues.best_time_slot: Second Saturday 11:00-13:00 after reopening is verified.
venues.why_fits_paints: South Norwood gives the Croydon list geographic counterweight and a more neighbourly rhythm than the town centre. The market has a community identity and is close to independent cafes and South Norwood Library. It remains Tier B because its live status is paused/reopening rather than reliably weekly.
venues.anecdote_story: Local coverage described the market as a South Norwood fixture with anniversary events and seasonal community trading.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Community market stalls; exact trader mix UNVERIFIED - host must confirm after reopening.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime only and keep the group close to Norwood Junction/Station Road footfall.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15-20 min by transit/walk; exceeds strict walking backup but can work as market-category transit backup if host accepts.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current Instagram reopening notice is the biggest operational blocker; do not publish before late Sept/early Oct confirmation.

venues.venue_name: Farthing Downs and Happy Valley Nature Trail
venues.opening_hours: City of London says Farthing Downs is open at all times; car park opens 7:00 and closes at dusk with seasonal gate times.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access approved Creative Walk site; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 if kept daytime, dry-weather, and led by a host comfortable with uneven ground.
venues.capacity_notes: 3-mile option via City/Surrey routes; unsurfaced paths, rough and muddy in places, cattle/sheep present.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 10:30-13:00, avoiding dusk and muddy winter conditions.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Croydon's cleanest Creative Walk fit because Farthing Downs is explicitly on the PAINTS safety-first shortlist. It has skyline views, chalk grassland, ancient field systems and a sense of leaving London without really leaving it. The route supports 3-4 story stops without forcing a risky or obscure path.
venues.anecdote_story: City of London notes panoramic skyline views, chalk grassland, orchids and yellow-rattle; local heritage sources note Saxon burial mounds and Iron Age field systems near Coulsdon South.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Suggested stops: Coulsdon South threshold; Farthing Downs ridge/viewpoint; ancient field-system/barrow interpretation; Happy Valley meadow edge. Use only the approved Farthing Downs portion if host is unsure about extending into Happy Valley.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Safety-First Shortlist route. Daytime only; uneven ground, livestock, mud and limited lighting mean no evening walk.
venues.backup_venue: No fully verified weather backup within 5 min of walk start; closest practical shelter is a Coulsdon cafe/pub near Coulsdon South, but PAINTS gates are UNVERIFIED.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather, livestock, mud, and lack of a verified 5-minute weather backup; host should recce and identify a cafe before launch.

venues.venue_name: Crystal Palace Park Dinosaurs and Terraces Walk
venues.opening_hours: Park open-access; market/cafe hours vary. Brown & Green Life cafe lists 8:30-16:30 weekdays and 8:30-17:00 weekends.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access approved Creative Walk park; cafe backup walk-in for groups is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for daytime 8-12 with a clear route and cafe meet point; avoid event days and dark park sections.
venues.capacity_notes: Large park route; use 3-4 stops and keep to high-footfall areas near the dinosaurs, terraces, Italian steps and cafe.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10:30-12:30, optionally paired with the park market.
venues.why_fits_paints: Crystal Palace Park is on the PAINTS safety-first shortlist and has one of London's strangest walk narratives: Victorian futurism, dinosaur sculptures, broadcast-tower skyline and ruined exhibition ambition. It is highly photogenic but familiar enough to incur an obviousness penalty. Tier B because the borough boundary is ambiguous and some parts sit outside Croydon proper.
venues.anecdote_story: The park's dinosaur sculptures and remnants of the Crystal Palace landscape make it a ready-made conversation route about spectacle, science and Victorian public culture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Suggested stops: Crystal Palace station/Brown & Green meet point; dinosaur lakes; terraces/Italian steps; transmitter skyline view.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Safety-First Shortlist park. Daytime only; avoid quieter wooded edges and do not route after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 0-5 min from Thicket Road / park start
venues.risks_watchouts: Croydon relevance is Upper Norwood-adjacent rather than purely borough-contained; weekend park crowds and weather need checking.

venues.opening_hours: Londonist lists Monday-Thursday 12:00-23:00, Friday-Saturday 12:00-00:00, Sunday 12:00-23:00; official about page confirms live music/events but current daily hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 seating without splitting is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm, especially around live music.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted drinks circle if a table/area is held; risky as pure walk-in on live music nights.
venues.capacity_notes: Character pub with live music and beer garden; group seating depends on programme and time.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 18:00-20:00 or Sunday afternoon jazz if host pre-checks seating.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Oval is the Croydon drinks pick with the most real story: residential-street location, live music culture, local beer and a proper community-pub feel. It is better for Show & Tell than sleek cocktail bars because the room itself gives people things to talk about. Tier B because wine/natural-wine evidence is limited and backup within 10 minutes is not strong.
venues.anecdote_story: Museum of Croydon notes the pub hosted multiple live music sessions weekly and supported emerging artists; Croydon BID describes it as hidden down a residential street and central to the borough's cultural scene.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rotating ales, local brews including Cronx references, organic wine options per Croydon BID, live music, quiz/storytime/community programming.
venues.safety_notes: Residential route near East Croydon; use well-lit main roads for arrival/departure and avoid very late solo dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min walk; no equally strong same-category backup found within strict 10 min
venues.risks_watchouts: Does not fully meet the independent wine-bar/natural-wine preference; host must check group seating and event noise.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram snippet lists Mon-Thu 09:00-21:00, Fri-Sat 09:00-22:00, Sun 09:00; official site is live and takes bookings, but holiday/group walk-in hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Booking available; walk-ins for 8-12 not verified. Treat as reservation-recommended and Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable if hosted with a held table; not robust as a no-notice walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Italian restaurant and wine bar; sociable boards/pinsa/pasta support group conversation.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday 18:00-20:00 for wine-focused format, avoiding Friday/Saturday peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Basil & Grape is not wildly off-grid, but it is one of Croydon's few live, central wine-led independents with enough operational heft for a small group. Italian wine labels, sharing boards and tastings give it more Show & Tell material than a generic cocktail bar. Tier B because the story is thinner and group walk-ins are not verified.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself as bringing Italian wine-bar culture to central Croydon, with handpicked Italian labels and rotating pasta.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Italian wines, cocktails, bruschetta, Italian sharing boards, pinsa pizza, pasta and wine tastings.
venues.safety_notes: Central George Street location near East Croydon/tram; use early evening rather than late-night weekend peak.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min walk; no same-category PAINTS-coded backup found within strict 10 min
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation-recommended, not a pure walk-in PAINTS model; confirm they can seat 8-12 without splitting.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Tempting Surrey Street cocktail-pub story, but live sources indicate the Croydon pub closed/relocated after 2025; cannot pass operational Gate 1.
Aesthetically strong former-Sainsbury's bar story, but multiple current sources list it as closed/closed long-term, so it fails Gate 1.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Anspach & Hobday Croydon Brewery / The Croydon Pigeon
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Bermondsey-born, Croydon-made brewery would be a good story, but official page says the Croydon Pigeon/taproom is temporarily closed.
Live taproom with useful hours, but it is primarily a brewery tap rather than independent wine bar/character gastro pub, and PAINTS backup/walk-in group evidence is thin.
Excellent independent bookshop with events and local identity, but no verified on-site cafe or literal next-door coffee partner; fails raised Books & Coffee rule despite strong bookshop credentials.
Warm community cafe with books/free groceries signal, but not a substantive bookshop/book-cafe; opening hours are weekday daytime and not enough for PAINTS Books & Coffee.
Independent gallery signal, but the visible exhibition page found was Modern-Day Art Exhibition ending 30 April 2026; no current July 2026 exhibition verified.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Art On Your Doorstep / National Gallery Croydon trail
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Compelling public art trail with National Gallery reproductions, but it ends 5 July 2026 and Creative Walks cannot use unapproved public-art routes beyond the v6 list; could be team-reviewed as a special one-off, not sourced as standard.
Locally relevant green-space route, but Coulsdon Common is explicitly on the removed PAINTS list, so it cannot be sourced or used as backup.
